
There are 2 important things to add when working on water, 1 is that that it needs collider, and 2 is that it needs to be set to the water layer. These can be found here

1: the colliders can be added to the object used as water, I would recommend you use a box collider, it tends to work better. When your water collider is a plane, if a plane flies though it fast enough, it can just fly under water technically with minimal damage. By making it a box collider, you get the same effects as if the plane splashes the water, and it slowly sinks downwards. When you create the water box (I normally use a default Unity white cube) make sure “is Trigger” and “Provides contact” is checked on, as you need it to yell at the plane for touching it

![](data:image/png;base64,)

The second thing you want to do is set the item under the water later, this can be found here at the top right of the inspector when you click on your water cube. This is an instruction mark inside the official Saccflight manual.

![](data:image/png;base64,)
